organic.title: Dark matter
organic.snippet: In astronomy and cosmology, dark matter is an invisible and hypothetical form of matter that does not interact with electromagnetic radiation, including light.

organic.title: Dark Matter
organic.snippet: Dark matter is the invisible glue that holds the universe together. This mysterious material is all around us, making up most of the matter in the universe.

organic.title: Dark matter
organic.snippet: Unlike normal matter, dark matter does not interact with the electromagnetic force. This means it does not absorb, reflect or emit light, making it extremely ...

organic.title: What Is Dark Matter?
organic.snippet: Dark matter is stuff in space that has gravity, but it is invisible and isn't like anything else we know about. Dark matter makes up about 27% of the universe.

organic.title: Dark matter | Definition, Discovery, Distribution, & Facts
organic.snippet: Dark matter, a component of the universe whose presence is discerned from its gravitational attraction rather than its luminosity.

organic.title: DOE Explains...Dark Matter
organic.snippet: Scientific evidence points to dark matter being a new type of fundamental particle or particles that interact with ordinary matter through gravity.

organic.title: Science 101: Dark Matter and Dark Energy
organic.snippet: We call it ​“dark” because we can't see it. Unlike visible matter (matter we can see, including stars, planets, water, etc.), it doesn't appear to release or ...
